basic_details.job_title: AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 83 Non-Academic Posts
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Written Examination (conditional) followed by Personal Interview, or Interview only
basic_details.short_information: AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 has been announced through Advertisement No. DEANAC-25012/1/2023-DEAN ACADEMIC/5842 dated 4 July 2026, inviting online applications from Indian citizens for 83 posts of Senior Resident (Non-Academic) across 34 departments for a tenure period of 3 years. Online applications open on 8 July 2026 and close on 22 July 2026 at aiimsbhubaneswar.nic.in.

important_dates.cut-off date for age, qualification & ex: Last date of submission of application form (22 July 2026)
important_dates.interview_date: To be informed on the AIIMS Bhubaneswar website

application_fee.general_fee: Rs 1,770 (Rs 1,500 + 18% GST) plus transaction charges as applicable
application_fee.sc_fee: Rs 1,416 (Rs 1,200 + 18% GST) plus transaction charges as applicable
application_fee.details: Mode of Payment: Debit Card / Credit Card / Net Banking. Fee once remitted will not be refunded under any circumstance; candidates are advised to satisfy themselves that they fulfil all eligibility criteria before filling up the application form. Candidates applying for more than one department (up to a maximum of 3) must deposit the application fee separately for each post/department.

age_limit.details: The upper age limit for applying to these posts is 45 years on the last date of application (Authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare Letter U.12016/10/2019-ME dated 2 January 2020). | The cut-off date to determine the maximum age limit, essential qualification, and experience will be the last date of submission of the application form.

qualification.details: Eligibility for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident (Non-Academic) Recruitment 2026 is primarily governed by the department-wise essential qualifications listed in the vacancy table above (Annexure-I), along with the following common conditions applicable to all departments. | Candidates must pass their qualifying examination by 31 August 2026, and the result must be declared to this effect on or before this date. The tenure of the qualifying degree must also be completing latest by 31 August 2026. Only candidates who have been/are declared successful in their qualifying degree exam/viva (DNB/MD/MS/DM/MCh, as applicable) and will be completing their tenure for the same on or before 31 August 2026 will be eligible to be considered. Candidates who have appeared for the exam/viva voce but whose result is yet to be declared will be allowed to appear in theory and/or interview provisionally; however, the result must be available by 31 August 2026. Where the result of the qualifying exam is declared after 31 August 2026, the candidature will stand cancelled, and no claim for selection based on the provisionally allowed theory exam/interview will be considered. Compulsory Tenure Days: 1095 days for MD/MS/MDS degree holders; 730 days for secondary DNB degree holders. Medical registration is mandatory before joining, if selected. Candidates who have completed 3 years of Residency under the Central Residency Scheme are not eligible to apply. The total tenure of Senior Residency (Non-Academic) is 3 years. This includes any period spent as a Senior Resident (Non-Academic) in the same specialty at any other teaching institution prior to joining AIIMS, Bhubaneswar. Candidates already in service in any Government Hospital/Institution must submit their application through proper channel only. They will not be permitted for interview if they fail to produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) on the day of interview during document scrutiny. They may be allowed provisionally only if they produce a certificate confirming that the HOD/Head of their employing organization has been informed. If it is later found that the candidate did not submit the application through proper channel, the candidature will stand cancelled, and if already selected, services will be terminated immediately.

salary_details.details: Selected Senior Residents (Non-Academic) will be paid Rs 67,700 as per 7th CPC, Level-11 of the Pay Matrix, plus Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) and other usual allowances as admissible under the rules.

selection_process: A candidate can apply for more than one post
up to a maximum of 3 departments
out of the departments listed in Annexure-I
but must apply separately for each post/department and deposit the application fee separately for each. No TA/DA will be paid to attend the interview

and any fee paid cannot be claimed back later. A written examination will only be conducted when the number of applicants is more than three times the number of posts advertised for that department. There will be no written test for posts where fewer applications are received than the number of vacancies. The Executive Director
has the discretion to decide for which posts a written examination will be conducted

and this decision is final. The merit list (selected and waiting list) for all categories – UR/EWS/OBC/SC/ST – will be prepared on the basis of combined marks of the written test and personal interview

or on personal interview alone (where no written test is held). Any vacancy arising due to non-joining or resignation of selected candidates will be offered to candidates from the waiting list as per the merit panel. All applicants must provide their latest mobile number and email address for timely communication. There is no provision for displaying marks of the written test/interview for rechecking

and no query in this regard will be entertained. Selected candidates are expected to stay in the Institute campus if offered residential accommodation. Candidates are advised to visit the AIIMS Bhubaneswar website regularly for the latest details on qualification
how_to_apply: Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria must apply through online mode only by visiting the official website aiimsbhubaneswar.nic.in (under the path Recruitment > Recruitment Notice > Online Recruitment), between 8 July 2026 and 22 July 2026. Applications submitted by any other mode will be rejected, and no further communication will be entertained.
Visit the Official Website – Go to aiimsbhubaneswar.nic.in and navigate to Recruitment > Recruitment Notice > Online Recruitment.
Fill the Online Application Form – Complete the application form with accurate personal, educational, and category details for the department(s) you are applying to (maximum 3).
Upload Required Documents – Upload scanned copies of all original certificates listed in the Documents Required section above.
Pay the Application Fee – Pay the applicable fee separately for each post/department via Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.

Submit and Download – Submit the application and download the filled application form along with the fee receipt for future reference and for submission at the time of document verification/interview.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Here are the key dates candidates need to track for the AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026.

content_sections.heading: Department-Wise Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: AIIMS Bhubaneswar Recruitment 2026 covers 83 Senior Resident (Non-Academic) vacancies spread across 34 departments, with both backlog and fresh category-wise splits as per Annexure-I of the official notification. Note: As per guidelines, EWS posts are not carried forward or considered as backlog vacancy if unfilled; other category candidates may be allowed to apply provisionally for such posts, subject to being considered as UR candidates only if the EWS seat is otherwise not filled. Given the complexity of this multi-column table, candidates are strongly advised to cross-check the exact category-wise split for their department of interest against Annexure-I of the official notification PDF before applying.

MS / DNB (General Surgery) OR MCh./DNB (Paediatric Surgery)
MS / DNB (General Surgery) OR MCh./DNB (Surgical Oncology)
MS/DNB (General Surgery) OR DNB/MCh in Burn & Plastic Surgery

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident (Non-Academic) Recruitment 2026 is primarily governed by the department-wise essential qualifications listed in the vacancy table above (Annexure-I), along with the following common conditions applicable to all departments. Candidates must pass their qualifying examination by 31 August 2026 , and the result must be declared to this effect on or before this date. The tenure of the qualifying degree must also be completing latest by 31 August 2026. Only candidates who have been/are declared successful in their qualifying degree exam/viva (DNB/MD/MS/DM/MCh, as applicable) and will be completing their tenure for the same on or before 31 August 2026 will be eligible to be considered. Candidates who have appeared for the exam/viva voce but whose result is yet to be declared will be allowed to appear in theory and/or interview provisionally; however, the result must be available by 31 August 2026. Where the result of the qualifying exam is declared after 31 August 2026, the candidature will stand cancelled, and no claim for selection based on the provisionally allowed theory exam/interview will be considered. Compulsory Tenure Days: 1095 days for MD/MS/MDS degree holders; 730 days for secondary DNB degree holders. Medical registration is mandatory before joining, if selected. Candidates who have completed 3 years of Residency under the Central Residency Scheme are not eligible to apply. The total tenure of Senior Residency (Non-Academic) is 3 years. This includes any period spent as a Senior Resident (Non-Academic) in the same specialty at any other teaching institution prior to joining AIIMS, Bhubaneswar. Candidates already in service in any Government Hospital/Institution must submit their application through proper channel only. They will not be permitted for interview if they fail to produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) on the day of interview during document scrutiny. They may be allowed provisionally only if they produce a certificate confirming that the HOD/Head of their employing organization has been informed. If it is later found that the candidate did not submit the application through proper channel, the candidature will stand cancelled, and if already selected, services will be terminated immediately.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit and Relaxation
content_sections.text: The upper age limit for applying to these posts is 45 years on the last date of application (Authority: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare Letter U.12016/10/2019-ME dated 2 January 2020). The cut-off date to determine the maximum age limit, essential qualification, and experience will be the last date of submission of the application form.

rows.category: Persons with Bench-mark Disabilities (PwBD) – General Category
Persons with Bench-mark Disabilities (PwBD) – OBC Category
Persons with Bench-mark Disabilities (PwBD) – SC/ST Category

content_sections.heading: Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected Senior Residents (Non-Academic) will be paid Rs 67,700 as per 7th CPC, Level-11 of the Pay Matrix, plus Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) and other usual allowances as admissible under the rules.

content_sections.heading: Reservation of Posts
content_sections.text: Reservation for OBC/SC/ST/EWS candidates is as per Central Government rules, with 4% reservation for Persons with Bench-mark Disabilities (PwBD), including Orthopedically Handicapped (OPH) candidates, on a horizontal basis. PwBD Criteria: The disability certificate must be signed by a duly constituted and authorized Medical Board of a State or Central Government Hospital/Institution, and countersigned by the Medical Superintendent/CMO/Head of the Hospital/Institution. The candidate will be allowed to join only after medical fitness certification by the duly constituted Medical Board, and must possess a valid document certifying their physical disability in conformity with the judgment of the Supreme Court of India. The Medical Board will include consultants from related disciplines. OBC Candidates: Must attach a certificate valid for posts under the Central Government of India confirming they do not belong to the Creamy Layer. The non-creamy layer certificate must be issued on or after 1 April 2026 and be valid for financial year 2026-27; failing which, the candidate will not be allowed to appear for interview in that category, though they may be treated as a UR candidate. EWS Candidates: Must possess a valid EWS certificate in Hindi/English, issued by the competent authority for admission/jobs in Central Government institutions, issued on or after 1 April 2026 and valid for financial year 2026-27. SC/ST Candidates: Certificates must be issued by authorities prescribed by the Government of India. Candidates who fail to produce a valid category certificate (OBC/EWS/SC/ST/PwBD) during document verification, or whenever required, will have their candidature cancelled. All candidates are advised to obtain the required valid category certificate in advance. The language of all certificates must be Hindi or English, in the prescribed format for the Central Government. All eligible applicants, irrespective of category, may also be considered for seats advertised under a reservation category. In case of non-availability of candidates in the category for which the seat has been advertised, candidates from other categories, including Unreserved, will be considered for appointment, subject to a limit not exceeding 44 days (extendable by the Competent Authority), strictly as per Government of India guidelines. As per guidelines, EWS posts are not carried forward or treated as backlog vacancy if unfilled; other category candidates may be allowed provisionally to apply for such posts, subject to being considered as UR only if the EWS seat is otherwise not filled.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Mode of Payment: Debit Card / Credit Card / Net Banking. Fee once remitted will not be refunded under any circumstance; candidates are advised to satisfy themselves that they fulfil all eligibility criteria before filling up the application form. Candidates applying for more than one department (up to a maximum of 3) must deposit the application fee separately for each post/department.

rows.application_fee: Rs 1,770 (Rs 1,500 + 18% GST) plus transaction charges as applicable
Rs 1,416 (Rs 1,200 + 18% GST) plus transaction charges as applicable

content_sections.heading: Selection Procedure
content_sections.text: A candidate can apply for more than one post, up to a maximum of 3 departments , out of the departments listed in Annexure-I, but must apply separately for each post/department and deposit the application fee separately for each. No TA/DA will be paid to attend the interview, and any fee paid cannot be claimed back later. A written examination will only be conducted when the number of applicants is more than three times the number of posts advertised for that department. There will be no written test for posts where fewer applications are received than the number of vacancies. The Executive Director, AIIMS Bhubaneswar, has the discretion to decide for which posts a written examination will be conducted, and this decision is final. The merit list (selected and waiting list) for all categories – UR/EWS/OBC/SC/ST – will be prepared on the basis of combined marks of the written test and personal interview, or on personal interview alone (where no written test is held). Any vacancy arising due to non-joining or resignation of selected candidates will be offered to candidates from the waiting list as per the merit panel. All applicants must provide their latest mobile number and email address for timely communication. There is no provision for displaying marks of the written test/interview for rechecking, and no query in this regard will be entertained. Selected candidates are expected to stay in the Institute campus if offered residential accommodation. Candidates are advised to visit the AIIMS Bhubaneswar website regularly for the latest details on qualification, reservation, selection procedure, emoluments, and date of interview.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Applicants must upload scanned copies of the following original certificates/documents to the online application form, wherever applicable: Date of Birth Certificate MBBS Degree Certificate Internship Completion Certificate Qualifying Degree Certificate (MBBS / BDS / MD / MS / MDS / DNB / DM / MCh, as applicable) Valid Medical Registration Certificate Relevant Category Certificate, if any (OBC/SC/ST/EWS/PwBD) Other relevant certificates, if any Tenure Completion Certificate Experience Certificate(s), if any No Objection Certificate (NOC), where applicable Candidates are advised to download the filled application form along with the fee receipt after submission, to be produced at the time of document verification/interview.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online
content_sections.text: Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria must apply through online mode only by visiting the official website aiimsbhubaneswar.nic.in (under the path Recruitment > Recruitment Notice > Online Recruitment ), between 8 July 2026 and 22 July 2026. Applications submitted by any other mode will be rejected, and no further communication will be entertained. Visit the Official Website – Go to aiimsbhubaneswar.nic.in and navigate to Recruitment > Recruitment Notice > Online Recruitment. Fill the Online Application Form – Complete the application form with accurate personal, educational, and category details for the department(s) you are applying to (maximum 3). Upload Required Documents – Upload scanned copies of all original certificates listed in the Documents Required section above. Pay the Application Fee – Pay the applicable fee separately for each post/department via Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking. Submit and Download – Submit the application and download the filled application form along with the fee receipt for future reference and for submission at the time of document verification/interview.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 83 vacancies for the post of Senior Resident (Non-Academic) across 34 departments at AIIMS, Bhubaneswar.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 22 July 2026. Online applications opened on 8 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident (Non-Academic) posts?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 45 years as on the last date of application, with relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and 10 to 15 years for PwBD candidates depending on category.

faq.question: Q4. What is the pay scale for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident (Non-Academic)?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will get Rs 67,700 as per 7th CPC Level-11 of the Pay Matrix, plus Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) and other usual allowances as admissible.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs 1,770 for General/OBC candidates, Rs 1,416 for SC/ST/EWS candidates, and no fee for PwBD candidates, payable via Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.

faq.question: Q6. What is the tenure period for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident (Non-Academic) posts?
faq.answer: The tenure period is 3 years, inclusive of any prior Senior Resident (Non-Academic) service in the same specialty at any other teaching institution.

faq.question: Q7. Can a candidate apply for more than one department?
faq.answer: Yes, a candidate can apply for a maximum of 3 departments, but must apply separately for each post/department and pay the application fee separately for each.

faq.question: Q8. Will there be a written exam for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A written examination will be conducted only if the number of applicants is more than three times the number of posts advertised for a department; otherwise, selection will be based on personal interview only. The Executive Director's decision on this is final.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply for AIIMS Bhubaneswar Senior Resident Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must apply online only through the official website aiimsbhubaneswar.nic.in, under Recruitment > Recruitment Notice > Online Recruitment. No other mode of application will be accepted.

faq.question: Q10. Are candidates who completed 3 years under the Central Residency Scheme eligible to apply?
faq.answer: No, candidates who have already completed 3 years of Residency under the Central Residency Scheme are not eligible to apply for this post.
